hypothesis: If diffusion-model generated crystal structures trained on MP/OQMD data (≤64 atoms, formation energy <0.1 eV/atom) pass DFT-based validation—low RMSD after relaxation, energy above hull ≤0.1 eV/atom, and valid bonding with minimum interatomic distances >0.5 Å—then these models reliably generate thermodynamically stable, chemically valid, and novel materials.
